title: Click Begin on the Quick Start Guide card.

sections.heading: Federation Management Console
sections.body:
You can manage federated authentication from the **Federation
Management Console**.

To open the **Federation Management** console:

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.body:
If you're configuring single sign-on for users in your organization for
the first time, you can follow MongoDB's federation management
Quick Start Guide.

To configure single sign-on with the Quick Start Guide:

- In Atlas, go to the Federation Management console for your organization.

  - If it's not already displayed, select your desired organization
    from the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

  - In the sidebar, click Federation under
    the Identity & Access heading.

  - Click Open Federation Management App.

    The [Federation](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%23%2Forg%2F%3Corganization%3E%2Ffederation) page
    displays.

- If it is not already displayed, open the
  Federation Management console Overview
  page using the left navigation panel.

  > **Note:** 
  >
  > Federation ID
  >
  > The Overview page displays a **Federation ID**, which is a
  > unique 24-character hexadecimal digit representing a specific
  > federation. This value is distinct for each **federation application**.

The Quick Start Guide walks you through the following:

- Adding and verifying your domains.

- Configuring your IdP (Identity Provider) with Atlas.

- Connecting your domains to your IdP (Identity Provider).

- Activating your IdP (Identity Provider) for users to access MongoDB.

Complete each of the four steps in order.

> **Note:** If you've already completed the federation management Quick Start
> but want to go through those steps again, click the link at the
> bottom of the **Federation Management** console
> **Overview** page.

sections.heading: Tutorials
sections.body:
To configure federated authentication from the **Federation
Management** console in Atlas, you must:

- Click **Manage Identity Providers** and [IdP to Atlas](atlas-manage-fed-auth) to ensure that
  your users are authenticated through your trusted
  IdP (Identity Provider).

- Click **Manage Domains** and [Manage Domain Mapping for Federated Authentication](atlas-manage-fed-domain-map) to simplify the
  login experience for users from specified domains. Atlas
  authenticates users through a mapped IdP (Identity Provider) if their email address
  matches a mapped domain.

After federating authentication, you can simplify user authorization.
In the **Federation Management** console, click
**Manage Organizations**. You can perform these activities:

- [Atlas organizations](atlas-manage-fed-org-map) to your
  IdP (Identity Provider).

- [Map Atlas roles](atlas-manage-fed-auth-roles) to groups in
  your IdP (Identity Provider).

End-to-end tutorials on implementing federated authentication:

- [Microsoft Entra ID](federated-auth-azure-ad)

- [Google Workspace](federated-auth-google-ws)

- [Okta](federated-auth-okta)

- [PingOne](federated-auth-ping-one)

sections.heading: Consideration for Restricted Access to Atlas UI
sections.body:
If you enable [Require IP Access List for the Atlas UI](atlas-ui-ip-access-list), and restrict access by any user to at least
one federated organization, Atlas doesn't allow this user to access
the **Federation Management** console.

sections.heading: Consideration for Two-Factor Authentication
sections.body:
Atlas bypasses 2FA (Two Factor Authentication) for users who authenticate with federated authentication
through your IdP (Identity Provider). If a user authenticates through your IdP (Identity Provider) and has 2FA (Two Factor Authentication)
for their Atlas account enabled, Atlas doesn't prompt the user for
2FA (Two Factor Authentication). Instead, you can configure your trusted IdP (Identity Provider) to prompt users for 2FA (Two Factor Authentication).
To learn more, see [enable multi-factor authentication](atlas-enable-mfa).
